description: The figure presents a machine learning feature importance analysis for Fe-C-Cr-Mn-Si steel design, divided into two conceptual panels. The left panel labeled "Data set" displays a grid of numerous small scatter plots or correlation matrices visualizing the training dataset (initially 173 points from MatWeb, augmented to 192 via CGANs), though individual plot details remain unreadable at this resolution. The right panel shows an "Importance ranking" funnel chart revealing the hierarchical influence of alloying elements on microhardness prediction. Chromium (Cr) is ranked at the top as the most significant element, followed by nickel (Ni) and molybdenum (Mo), with additional elements indicated by ellipsis at the bottom. This ranking derives from comprehensive feature engineering including Recursive Feature Elimination (RFE) voting, Pearson correlation analysis, and exhaustive screening combined with solid solution strengthening theory. The visualization supports the core conclusion that Cr, Ni, and Mo positively impact steel hardness, while elements like Mn, V, and W show weaker correlations, guiding the inverse design optimization to achieve predicted hardness up to 733.59 HV.
